mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent monthly inflation above 3% (CPIAUCSL) signals central bank inaction, leading to real rate compression and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors price in delayed tightening.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns remain negative in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.0 for 3+ consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in unemployment (UNRATE) and increases in payroll employment (PAYEMS) signal tight labor markets, driving corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between UNRATE decline and PAYEMS growth shows negative coefficient in 3-year rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly industrial production (INDPRO) growth above 1% signals expanding capacity utilization, reducing recession risk and generating positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO growth exceeds 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate hikes above 4.5% signal tightening cycles, increasing discount rates and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as risk assets face higher financing costs.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) above 3% indicates strong macroeconomic momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through improved corporate earnings visibility.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are negative in 50%+ of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in unemployment (UNRATE) and declines in housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening demand, creating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through recession risk repricing.
failure_condition: Interaction term between UNRATE increase and HOUST decline shows positive coefficient in 3-year rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in consumer spending (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates robust demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through improved corporate revenue outlooks.
failure_condition: Interaction term between RSAFS growth and HOUST increase shows negative coefficient in 3-year rolling regressions.
